scope: specFolder, packet_id, spec_folder path-shape inconsistency across generators and DB consumers
summary: description.json uses caller-base-relative paths that can include .opencode/specs, graph metadata strips to specs-root-relative. Many DB consumers compare exact spec_folder = ?, so alias forms fragment dedup, list, health, and learning scopes.

title: Shared canonicalizer for spec-folder identity
problem: description.json and graph-metadata.json use different path shapes. generate-description stores path.relative(basePath, folderPath) directly, while graph metadata resolves to specs-root-relative. Live description.json carries .opencode/specs prefixes and parentChain includes .opencode and specs.
evidence: generate-description.ts:77-84, folder-discovery.ts:883-895, graph strip graph-metadata-parser.ts:653-667 and :1097-1129
proposal: Add one helper in spec-doc-paths.ts that canonicalizes any absolute, specs/, .opencode/specs/, or root-relative value to specs-root-relative. Use it in both generators. Overlaps with a1-f4.

title: Normalize MCP inputs before DB use
problem: MCP schemas reject traversal but not path aliases. Many consumers compare exact spec_folder = ?, so alias forms fragment list, health, learning, and dedup scopes. A local normalizer exists in discovery but is not shared.
evidence: tool-input-schemas.ts:55-64 and :168-190, list memory-crud-list.ts:122-124, health memory-crud-health.ts:955-957, learning session-learning.ts:340-349, dedup save/dedup.ts:94-110, local normalizer memory-index-discovery.ts:92-108
proposal: Transform every public specFolder input through the shared canonicalizer at the schema or handler boundary, and normalize parsed metadata before DB writes in memory-parser.ts. Needs a migration or compatibility query for persisted rows.

title: Make generator base resolution fail-closed
problem: generate-description trusts caller basePath after containment only, so using the repo root makes specFolder include .opencode/specs. The workflow retries multiple bases when regenerating missing descriptions, and config orders specs before .opencode/specs.
proposal: Derive the containing specs root from folderPath instead of accepting arbitrary basePath, or reject a basePath that is not an approved specs root. Call this resolver once in workflow regeneration.

title: Add generated-JSON path invariants
problem: Schemas allow any non-empty string for path identity: specFolder is z.string, packet_id and spec_folder are z.string().min(1), so future regressions pass validation.
proposal: Add schema refinements or a validator that rejects specFolder, spec_folder, packet_id, parent_id, and relationship spec_folder values starting with /, specs/, or .opencode/specs/, and rejects parentChain entries .opencode and specs. Overlaps with a9 validator.
